captures.title: Orma — Your memory layer for the web
captures.pageText: Memory Chat Analysis + screenshots from your browsing Hi! I'm your Orma memory assistant. Ask me anything about what you've browsed and I'll give you a detailed answer with screenshots as evidence — even days later. how many api keys are created in groq portal and with what names? I found 4 captures related to "api keys": • "API Keys - GroqCloud" on console.groq.com at Jul 15, 10:36 AM • "API Keys - GroqCloud" on console.groq.com at Jul 15, 10:35 AM • "Orma — Your memory layer for the web" on localhost at Jul 15, 10:36 AM • "salon figma design (Community) – Figma" on figma.com at Jul 15, 10:35 AM ⚡ Add your Groq API key to get full AI analysis including screenshot reading. 📎 SOURCES FROM YOUR MEMORY (4) [1] GENERAL API Keys - GroqCloud Wed, Jul 15, 10:36 AM console.groq.com [2] GENERAL API Keys - GroqCloud Wed, Jul 15, 10:35 AM console.groq.com [3] GENERAL Orma — Your memory layer for the web Wed, Jul 15, 10:36 AM localhost [4] GENERAL salon figma design (Community) – Figma Wed, Jul 15, 10:35 AM figma.com

captures.title: Orma — Your memory layer for the web
captures.pageText: ഓ Orma How it works Features Dashboard Sign out CHROME EXTENSION · IN EARLY ACCESS You already read it once. You shouldn't have to find it again. Orma saves what you read, understands it in the background, and lets you ask for it back in plain language — weeks later, without the tab. Add to Chrome — it's free See how it works pinecone.io/learn/vector-databases ഓ Vector Databases, Explained Saved to RAG Research · just now Bookmarks are where research goes to be forgotten. Notes apps live outside the browser, so saving anything takes an extra step you skip on a deadline. What you read stays trapped on the one tab you already closed. What Orma actually does Four things, working together in the background while you keep browsing. One-click save Click the Orma icon on any page. It cleans the article and stores it — no folders to pick. Ask, don’t search Describe what you remember in plain words. Orma finds the page, even if you forgot the title. Chat with your reading Ask a question that spans several saved pages and get one answer, with sources linked. Groups itself Related pages land in the same workspace automatically, from the first page you save. How it works Step 1 Save Click the Orma icon. The page is cleaned and stored in seconds. Step 2 Understand Orma reads it in the background and files it with related pages. Step 3 Recall Search in your own words, or ask the chat panel a question directly. Start remembering what you read. Free while in early access. Takes under a minute to set up. Add to Chrome — it's free Orma (ഓർമ്മ) — Malayalam for “memory.”
captures.summary: Orma is a browser extension that saves and retrieves information from the web, allowing users to easily find and recall previously read content without having to search for it again
captures.visualDescription: This is a landing page for Orma, a browser extension or application that helps users save and retrieve information from the web. The page features a hero section with a tagline "You shouldn't have to find it again" and a brief description of Orma's functionality, along with two call-to-action buttons: "Add to Chrome — it's free" and "See how it works". The user is likely visiting this page to learn more about Orma and potentially install the browser extension.
